normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem binary_search_success (a : Arr) (t : Int) (i : Nat)
  (h : binarySearch a t = some i) :
  i < a.length ∧ a.get? i = some t := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the function returns Some i, then i is in range and arr[i] equals the target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem binary_search_absent_when_sorted (a : Arr) (t : Int)
  (hs : Sorted a) (h : binarySearch a t = none) :
  ∀ i, i < a.length → a.get? i ≠ some t := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assuming the input is sorted in nondecreasing order, if the function returns None then the target does not occur anywhere in the slice.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem binary_search_terminates (a : Arr) (t : Int) :
  ∃ r : SearchResult, r = binarySearch a t := by
  refine ⟨binarySearch a t, rfl⟩
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The function always returns a result value (terminates with an output on every input).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem binary_search_duplicate_indices (a : Arr) (t : Int) (i j : Nat)
  (hi : binarySearch a t = some i) (hj : binarySearch a t = some j) :
  i < a.length ∧
  j < a.length ∧
  a.get? i = some t ∧
  a.get? j = some t := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If duplicates exist, any two Some-results returned for the same search must both be valid hits for the target.
